sync_type: Bidirectional (Zapier acts as middleware reacting to Calendly events and triggering actions in other tools)
popularity: very high — most common Calendly integration pattern: booking triggers CRM/HubSpot contact creation via Zapier

authentication_requirements.zapier_side: OAuth 2.0 per connected app. Zapier manages auth on behalf of the user.
authentication_requirements.calendly_side: OAuth 2.0 or Personal Access Token (PAT). Webhook subscriptions scope required.

common_use_cases: Calendly event booked → Zapier creates HubSpot/Salesforce contact
Calendly event rescheduled/canceled → Zapier updates CRM status
Calendly invitee questions → Zapier creates deal with custom fields
Calendly event type → Zapier routing to different CRM pipelines
Calendly webhook → Zapier sends Slack notification to sales rep

zapier_endpoints.purpose: Zapier internal API; user doesn't call directly

calendly_endpoints.purpose: Create webhook subscription for event types
Get event details with invitee, guest info

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly sends webhook signature header for verification. Zapier's built-in Calendly trigger handles this, but custom webhook trigger doesn't.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use Zapier's Calendly integration (handles verification). If using Webhooks by Zapier, verify the signature manually using Calendly's webhook signing secret.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly delivers webhooks asynchronously. High-traffic events may have 30-120 second delays. Zapier scheduled poll adds 1-15 min more.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: For real-time needs, use Calendly webhook (instant) → Zapier Catch Hook trigger (instant). Avoid Zapier polling triggers.

edge_cases_and_errors.error_code: Calendly API rate limit on webhook subscriptions
edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly enforces rate limits on API calls. Frequent webhook subscription creation/deletion triggers 429.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Reuse webhook subscriptions. Monitor rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining). Cache subscription IDs.

edge_cases_and_errors.error_code: Multiple Calendly event types — single Zap limitation
edge_cases_and_errors.cause: One Calendly trigger in Zapier watches 'all event types' by default. Users need different actions per event type (e.g., discovery call → Salesforce lead, demo → HubSpot contact).
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use 'Filter by Zapier' step to route based on event type name. Or create separate Zaps per event type.

edge_cases_and_errors.error_code: Calendly invitee time zone not passed to CRM
edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly collects invitee timezone during booking. If Zapier step doesn't map timezone field to CRM, the timezone data is lost.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Map 'invitee timezone' field from Calendly trigger to CRM custom 'Timezone' field. Don't rely on automatically detected timezone.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly sends 'event.created' on initial booking and another 'event.created' (not 'event.rescheduled') on reschedule. Zapier creates duplicate CRM records.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use Calendly 'event.created' but check for existing contact/lead by email before creating. Add dedup logic in Zapier step.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly send 'event.canceled' webhook. Many Zaps process 'event.created' only, missing the cancel event. CRM shows appointments that were cancelled.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Add a Zap that listens for 'event.canceled' and updates CRM record status to 'No Show' or 'Cancelled'.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Calendly OAuth access tokens expire. Zapier handles this automatically, but custom setup connecting Calendly API directly via Webhooks by Zapier needs manual token refresh.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use Zapier's built-in Calendly integration (managed OAuth). If using Webhooks by Zapier, implement token refresh logic.

community_solutions.solution: Calendly caps webhook payloads. Large invitee question sets may be truncated. Send invitee UUID and fetch full details via API.
Calendly delivers 'at least once.' Add idempotency check in Zapier using webhook UUID.
